steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content:
A quadratic has no real solutions when the discriminant is negative. For $x^2-36x+c=0$, the discriminant is
$$
(-36)^2-4(1)(c)=1{,}296-4c
$$

steps.title: Find the cutoff
steps.content: Set the discriminant equal to $0$ to find the boundary: $$ 1{,}296-4c=0 $$ $$ 4c=1{,}296 $$ For no real solutions, $c$ must be greater than this cutoff. The answer is 324.
